sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presents with neck pain that started after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports the pain is sharp, incapacitating, and a 12 out of 10 in severity.
He has a history of multiple car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery, and has been experiencing pain and stiffness in the neck since the most recent crash.
He reports fatigue and difficulty moving his neck, with pain on palpation of the bony process and muscle.
He also reports numbness in the left arm and right leg, as well as spasms in his body.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery.
He has also been to therapy and a pain clinic, where he was prescribed fentanyl, which he has not taken in several weeks.
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, and visual disturbances, but these have not been recent.
He denies fever, but reports bruising and swelling, which he has experienced "sometimes".
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has moderate range of movement in his neck, with pain on palpation of the bony process and muscle.
He is unable to move his neck forward or backward without pain.
There is no bruising or swelling noted on the physical exam.
Test Results: The patient had an x-ray prior to coming in, which did not show a fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is a condition characterized by injury to the neck muscles and ligaments.
Problem list: Neck sprain; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg orally every 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation and pain management.
I also recommended that the patient apply ice to the affected area when experiencing bruising and swelling, and perform exercises to maintain range of movement in the neck.
The patient will undergo an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy for additional treatment.
I will also refer the patient to pain management for consideration of local injections.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the MRI results are available, at which time we will reassess the plan and determine the need for further treatment.
The patient will be given a note for work to excuse him from work until further notice.
